feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The older you get, the better you were.
Lucky Numbers: 4, 63, 60, 48, 55, 9

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Do something unusual when locking the door before a long trip to remember that you've definitely done it.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Americans on the average eat 18 acres of pizza every day.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Gustave Flaubert
entry.content: “Success is a consequence and must not be a goal.” — Gustave Flaubert

entry.title: ‘All smiles.’ Mariners select college slugger Ace Reese in first round of MLB Draft
entry.content: “I definitely had (the Mariners) on my list of teams I wanted to go to, so I was pumped when I got that opportunity,” Reese said.

entry.title: Nations Championship schedule, results, kick-off times and how to watch
entry.content: Everything you need to know ahead of the inaugural edition of the competition

entry.title: Jake Schaffner drafted by Boston Red Sox in 1st round of MLB Draft
entry.content: Former North Carolina shortstop Jake Schaffner became the 26th Tar Heel taken in the first round when the Boston Red Sox selected him No. 20 overall.

entry.title: Collegiate baseball: Rapids generate big rally against Genesee to win season home finale
entry.content: WATERTOWN — Even when they’re down, the Watertown Rapids have rarely considered themselves out for most of their challenging season. Despite blowing an early lead, the Rapids responded by finding a way to finally break through for a victory, winning in their season home finale Friday night. After the Genesee Rapids won the first game of the day, 9-3, Watertown bounced back to prevail, 10-8, in ...

entry.title: Reds $15 million slugger tabbed as 'buy low' trade target for 49 homer reason
entry.content: This expensive slugger would make a lot of sense as a trade chip this summer.

entry.title: Detroit Tigers select RHP Evan Dempsey at No. 69 overall in 2026 MLB Draft
entry.content: The Detroit Tigers drafted RHP Evan Dempsey with the No. 69 overall pick in the 2026 MLB Draft on Saturday in Philadelphia.

entry.title: Dodgers draft Bo Lowrance with 1st pick, 40th overall
entry.content: The Dodgers began their 2026 MLB Draft on Saturday by selecting Bo Lowrance, a high school third baseman from South Carolina with their first-round pick, and 40th overall in the draft. By virtue of winning the World Series last year, the Dodgers owned the 30th pick in the first round this year. But by exceeding […]

entry.title: Ole Miss signee Cole Prosek picked by Chicago White Sox in 2026 MLB Draft
entry.content: Who drafted Cole Prosek? The Chicago White Sox picked the Ole Miss signee in the second round of the 2026 MLB Draft. Here's what to expect from him.

entry.title: Poll: Americans split on capitalism, skeptical of democracy
entry.content: Americans' views on capitalism are split but lean negative, according to a new survey. Just over half of Americans, 51%, believe capitalism is functioning "not too well" or "not at all well," while 48% say it is functioning "very well" or "somewhat well."

entry.title: Why Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) Is Well Positioned to Capitalize on Hyperscale AI Networking Demand With Its Expanding Optical Order Backlog
entry.content: Why Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) Is Well Positioned to Capitalize on Hyperscale AI Networking Demand With Its Expanding Optical Order Backlog

entry.title: 28 beluga whales relocating to U.S. aquariums from shuttered Canadian theme park
entry.content: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration has approved plans to relocate 28 beluga whales from a closed Canadian theme park.

entry.title: Guggenheim Museum among NYC buildings that tested positive for Legionnaires' amid disease outbreak
entry.content: New York City's famed Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum was among a number of Manhattan buildings that recently tested positive for the bacteria that causes Legionnaires' disease amid the city's latest outbreak.

entry.title: When is Wimbledon men’s final? Jannik Sinner v Alexander Zverev start time and how to watch
entry.content: Sinner will attempt to win consecutive Wimbledon titles while Zverev is out to win a second grand slam title in a row

entry.title: McGlynn flattered by Scotland links but focused on Falkirk
entry.content: Falkirk boss John McGlynn is flattered to be touted for the Scotland job but insists he is fully focused on the Bairns. After back-to-back promotions that saw Falkirk rise from League One to the Scottish Premiership in two years, the 64-year-old led the side to a top-six finish in their first season back in the top flight. Given the impressive job McGlynn has done, former Scotland boss Craig Levein suggested McGlynn would be an excellent pick to succeed Steve Clarke as the national team manager.

entry.title: Why a two-week transformation now poses Wimbledon final’s biggest question
entry.content: Jannik Sinner, the world No 1 and reigning Wimbledon champion, has a dominant record against Alexander Zverev, but has becoming a grand slam winner changed everything for the German?

entry.title: De Bruyne reassures Napoli fans after Belgium World Cup exit: ‘I’ll be back’
entry.content: Kevin De Bruyne has reassured Napoli supporters that he will come back stronger next season, hours after Belgium’s World Cup ended in quarter-final heartbreak.The Red Devils bowed out following a ti...

entry.title: What Arkansas baseball’s Ryder Helfrick brings to Diamondbacks in MLB Draft
entry.content: Ryder Helfrick is headed to Arizona. Here's what the Arkansas baseball catcher brings to the Diamondbacks in the first round of the MLB Draft.

entry.title: Meta removes AI feature that drew from users' public Instagram content
entry.content: Meta has axed a feature in its new AI image generation model that allowed users on Instagram to create images drawing on the publicly available media of other users.

entry.title: These underperforming trades could yield big returns over next six months
entry.content: ETF Action's Mike Akins is encouraging investors to boost exposure to groups that underperformed compared with major artificial intelligence stocks.

entry.title: Dating and immigration: Apps don't ask for a green card, and some men are banking on that
entry.content: Dating is hard enough without throwing illegal immigration into the equation.

entry.title: Could birthright citizenship be the next Roe v. Wade?
entry.content: It took nearly 50 years for conservatives to see Roe v. Wade overturned. They're hoping it won't take that long to nix Trump v. Barbara, last month's decision on birthright citizenship.

entry.title: Unsealed letter reveals Scalia's fierce defense of landmark religious liberty decision
entry.content: Justice Antonin Scalia usually let his opinions speak for themselves, but he was so irked by criticism of one of his religious liberty rulings that he wrote a private defense of his reasoning, according to an unsealed court document obtained by "Seen, Heard & Whispered."

entry.title: Apple files lawsuit accusing ChatGPT maker OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
entry.content: Apple on Friday accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets as it seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT, a major rupture in a partnership between the iPhone maker and the artificial intelligence company.

entry.title: Bluesky has an official CEO again
entry.content: Toni Schneider, who took over after Jay Graber stepped down, is now Bluesky's official CEO.

entry.title: Japan's space agency conducts first test flight for experimental reusable rocket
entry.content: Japan's experimental reusable rocket took off and safely landed in a first test flight Saturday as the country seeks to achieve the technology key to cut launch costs and compete in the global space market dominated by SpaceX.

entry.title: Four NYT journalists subpoenaed days after report questioned Air Force One security
entry.content: The Trump administration has subpoenaed several New York Times journalists after their report on security concerns involving the new Air Force One, according to the paper.

entry.title: Who needs scalpers when GameStop is marking up Pokémon cards by more than 300 percent?
entry.content: Is paying a reasonable price for colorful cardboard too much to ask?

entry.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
entry.content: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents sayNigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official.Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. Continue reading...

entry.title: 'I hugged him tightly' - Courtois backs Lammens, wants Belgium break
entry.content: Belgium goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois still had a job to do after being substituted with injury in Friday's World Cup quarter-final defeat to Spain - console his replacement Senne Lammens. The Manchester United keeper, who had an impressive first season in England, spilled a long-range shot from Pau Cubarsi on 88 minutes and allowed Mikel Merino to grab Spain's late winner for 2-1.

entry.title: Barcelona World Cup Spotlight: Cubarsi, Lamine Yamal impress for Spain vs Belgium
entry.content: The second quarter-final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup took place last night, with Spain facing Belgium.Both teams knew that a win would secure their place in the semi-finals, where France await, and La ...

entry.title: Erling Haaland and Jude Bellingham on same team: Revisiting the bromance from 2020-22 Borussia Dortmund

entry.title: Conor McGregor 'billionaire strut', explained: How UFC star popularized the swagger walk

entry.title: Matt Olson plays in 741st straight game with Braves to become the franchise's Iron Man
entry.content: Atlanta Braves first baseman Matt Olson made franchise history on Friday night against the St. Louis Cardinals when he played in his 741st consecutive game for the team to break Dale Murphy’s record of 740 set from 1981 to 1986. Olson signed with Atlanta as a free agent ahead of the 2022 season after playing parts of six seasons with the Oakland Athletics. “It’s quite an accomplishment,” Braves manager Walt Weiss said.

entry.title: England’s tortured relationship with quarter-finals is over – but beating Norway would break new ground
entry.content: England shattered their old glass ceiling with successive deep runs under Gareth Southgate, writes Richard Jolly, and are back at this telling stage with Thomas Tuchel at the 2026 World Cup

entry.title: Who could take a penalty for England if World Cup quarter-final against Norway goes to a shootout?
entry.content: England have won only one penalty shootout at a World Cup before but Thomas Tuchel predicts they will need to win two to triumph in North America this summer

entry.title: Kirst-Sowers duo powers Waterdogs past Redwoods in statement win
entry.content: The Philadelphia Waterdogs’ thrilling 15-13 victory over the California Redwoods on Friday might have been the Premier Lacrosse League’s game of the year so far. The bigger story, though, was...

entry.title: Wildin’! Conor McGregor shows up in lifts with neck hickies for UFC 329 ceremonial weigh-ins
entry.content: There’s nothing like a Conor McGregor ceremonial weigh-in, and UFC 329 was extra special given it was McGregor’s first in five years. McGregor is set to face Max Holloway for the second time after beating “Blessed” in dominant fashion 13 years ago. A lot has changed since then. Holloway went on to be a dominant […]

entry.title: Report: Former Gonzaga guard Malachi Smith waived by Brooklyn Nets
entry.content: Jul. 10—Malachi Smith was rock-solid for the Brooklyn Nets during a 15-game stint to close the 2025-26 NBA season, but the former Gonzaga guard won't be on the team's roster next season after being waived Friday. The Nets exercised Smith's minimum-salary team option at the end of June, but elected to move on from the 26-year-old who spent the majority of the season with Brooklyn's G League ...

entry.content: The Braves and Cardinals are playing in St. Louis, so of course there’s a rain delay.

entry.title: Dodgers vs. D-backs game chat
entry.content: The Dodgers (61-33) take on the D-backs (46-47) at Dodger Stadium Friday night for a NL West clash. Shohei Ohtani was scratched from his start due to continued left knee irritation. Kyle Hurt will open a bullpen game for the Dodgers in the series opener. Eduardo Rodriguez (7-3, 2.25 ERA, 1.16 WHIP) goes for the […]

entry.title: Arthur Fery's Cinderella run ends in Wimbledon semifinal match to Alexander Zverev
entry.content: Championship weekend at Wimbledon has arrived, but it will not include the "Fery-tale" British matchup that tennis fans had been hoping for. Matthew Futterman, senior writer for tennis at The Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to preview the gentlemen's singles final.

entry.title: Judge vacates convictions of 4 Proud Boys in Jan. 6 Capitol insurrection
entry.content: A federal judge on Friday agreed to dismiss the convictions of four members of the far-right Proud Boys group for their actions in the Jan. 6 Capitol riot.
